source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A ruby laser emits light with a wavelength of $6943 \AA$, which to a very good approximation is a plane wave. What are the amplitudes of the electric and magnetic field vectors of this plane wave propagating through water, if the time-averaged power of the beam in the water is 100 milliwatts $/ \mathrm{cm}^{2}$ ?
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object representation rather than a file path or URL
record includes image/caption metadata, but the question text does not explicitly reference an image and the caption appears unrelated to the question

image_dependency: 题干为纯文字物理题，未引用图像、图表或空间位置关系。
输入字段 `requires_image=false` 且 `text_dominant=true`，直接表明解题不依赖图像。
虽存在 1 张已登记图片，但题目内容本身未显示需要读取该图片信息。
multi_step: 需要把给定的时间平均功率密度与平面波场强关系对应起来，再分别求电场和磁场振幅。
题目同时涉及介质（水）中的传播，通常需要结合介质中的波速/阻抗关系，而不是单步直接读数。
答案为两个物理量的数值集合，说明解答过程至少包含连续的公式应用与单位处理。
verifiability: 标准答案是明确的数值集合（电场振幅、磁场振幅），输出形式清晰。
题干给出完整已知量（波长、介质、平均功率密度），可通过物理公式独立复核。
`answer_present=true`、`answer_type=set`、`registry_passed=true`，说明样本结构完整，便于后续规则核验。

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A ruby laser emits light with a wavelength of $6943 \AA$, which to a very good approximation is a plane wave. What are the amplitudes of the electric and magnetic field vectors of this plane wave propagating through water, if the time-averaged power of the beam in the water is 100 milliwatts $/ \mathrm{cm}^{2}$ ?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object representation rather than a file path or URL
record includes image/caption metadata, but the question text does not explicitly reference an image and the caption appears unrelated to the question
metadata.reasoning_chain: For a plane electromagnetic wave, $\sqrt{\epsilon} E=\sqrt{\mu} H$. The time-averaged power of the beam per unit cross-sectional area is its intensity $$I=\langle E H\rangle=\sqrt{\frac{\epsilon}{\mu}}\left\langle E^{2}\right\rangle=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{\frac{\epsilon}{\mu}} E_{0}^{2}=\frac{n}{2 c \mu} E_{0}^{2},$$ where $E_{0}$ is the amplitude of the electric field, $c$ the velocity of light in free space and $n$ the refractive index of water. The permeability of water is $$\mu \approx \mu_{0}=4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{H} / \mathrm{m} .$$ Hence $$\begin{array}{l} E_{0}=\sqrt{\frac{2 I c \mu}{n}}=7.5 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m} \\ B_{0}=\frac{E_{0}}{v}=\frac{E_{0} n}{c}=3.3 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~T}, \end{array}$$ $B_{0}$ being the amplitude of the magnetic induction.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: For a plane electromagnetic wave, $\sqrt{\epsilon} E=\sqrt{\mu} H$. The time-averaged power of the beam per unit cross-sectional area is its intensity $$I=\langle E H\rangle=\sqrt{\frac{\epsilon}{\mu}}\left\langle E^{2}\right\rangle=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{\frac{\epsilon}{\mu}} E_{0}^{2}=\frac{n}{2 c \mu} E_{0}^{2},$$ where $E_{0}$ is the amplitude of the electric field, $c$ the velocity of light in free space and $n$ the refractive index of water. The permeability of water is $$\mu \approx \mu_{0}=4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{H} / \mathrm{m} .$$ Hence $$\begin{array}{l} E_{0}=\sqrt{\frac{2 I c \mu}{n}}=7.5 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m} \\ B_{0}=\frac{E_{0}}{v}=\frac{E_{0} n}{c}=3.3 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~T}, \end{array}$$ $B_{0}$ being the amplitude of the magnetic induction.
